name: v05-github-webhook-router
description: GitHub webhook receiver. Trigger-level middleware verifies the X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC against GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET; only verified deliveries reach the body. The switch then routes by X-GitHub-Event header to one of three per-event sub-workflows: push → v05-github-handler-push; the pull-request family (pull_request, pull_request_review, pull_request_review_comment) → v05-github-handler-pr; the issue family (issues, issue_comment) → v05-github-handler-issues. Anything unknown logs and returns 200 (GitHub retries non-2xx, so we don't want to fail unknown events). Demonstrates: trigger-level middleware (HMAC verify), switch with array `when` for grouping related events, default fallback that's a no-op log, sub-workflow dispatch with inputs forwarded as the child request body.

inputs.message: js/`github-webhook: unknown event type '${ctx.request.headers['x-github-event'] || ''}', ignoring (returning 200 to avoid GitHub retry storm)`

inputs.expression: ((dispatchKey) => { const childResp = dispatchKey ? ctx.state[dispatchKey] : null; const childData = childResp && childResp.data ? childResp.data : childResp; return { received: true, eventType: ctx.request.headers['x-github-event'] || null, deliveryId: ctx.request.headers['x-github-delivery'] || null, dispatchedTo: dispatchKey || 'log-unknown-event', child: childData }; })(['dispatch-push','dispatch-pr','dispatch-issues'].find(k => ctx.state[k] !== undefined))
